name: Lower Silesian Voivodeship (Poland)

colors.symbolism: outline and details of the Silesian eagle

description: Yellow field charged at centre with the black eagle of Lower Silesia, bearing a white crescent and cross-charge on its chest.
history: Adopted in 2006 by the Lower Silesian Voivodeship, restoring the medieval heraldic device of the Silesian Piasts.

features.symbolism: the heraldic field colour of Lower Silesia

features.role: Lower Silesian black eagle, with white crescent and cross-charge on its chest
features.symbolism: the historic Piast emblem of the Duchy of Silesia
